channel.title: Procurement on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Procurement on RaihnForge

item.title: The Buyer's Experience
item.description:
A note on naming. The original paper named the specific vendor and salesperson. For public posting, both have been generalized: “the vendor” and “the sales representative.” The analysis is preserved verbatim. The intent was always portfolio reflection on the frameworks, not commentary on any individual professional.

TO: Robert Lupton FROM: Joshua Keyes DATE: June 2, 2023 SUBJECT: The Buyer’s Experience, Final

Product Information

One of my responsibilities as project manager for my employer is assisting in the vetting of potential vendors that are involved in our SOC 2 certification project. I was responsible for leading the search for the needed auditing firm that would be a good fit for our organization’s specialization and processes. We have already been utilizing a compliance monitoring platform as a consulting and monitoring vendor for certification readiness. When it came time for audit selection, that platform recommended several firms to us, and the vendor described below was one of them. I scheduled an appointment and met with the vendor as part of our procurement process on Friday, November 4, at ten in the morning. The sales representative was the point of contact. The meeting was conducted virtually through Zoom (Keyes, 2022).

item.title: Understanding Contracts: CPIF vs FPIF and Procurement Strategy
item.description:
Editor’s note: This is student-era work from May 2022. The contract math in Part 1 has some confused arithmetic (sharing ratios applied as straight divisions, totals that don’t reconcile against the stated targets). It’s preserved as written so the paper reads as the original calculation walkthrough; treat Part 1 as the artifact, not the answer key.

Part 1. Working the Contract Scenarios

1. Cost Plus Incentive Fee Contract

Parameter Value
Target Cost $150,000
Target Fee $25,000
Target Price $175,000
Sharing Ratio 80/20 (80% buyer, 20% seller)
Actual Cost $140,000

Additional Fee = $0. Total Fee Paid = $25,000. Actual cost is under target cost so the incentive fee is awarded.
